12 reps · 1 rep each · 3 min FULL rest between EVERY rep
WHY: 6s sprint = 94% ATP-PCr energy. 3 min rest = >95% PCr resynthesised. This trains alactic power AND aerobic PCr resynthesis rate = game-5 first-step speed.
  • From squash ready position: crouch, weight forward, feet split
  • Drive back leg through floor — first step is everything
  • MAXIMUM effort — 100%, not 95%
  • Stop at 6 seconds — do not continue
  • 3 min timer between every rep — use sidebar
  • Stop session if any rep feels >10% slower than your best rep

10m acceleration

5 reps · 3–4 min full rest each
WHY: First-step + continuation speed development.
  • Same mechanics — push through to 10m
  • Full explosive push from start
  • Full recovery every rep — same quality rules apply

Lateral first-step (from T-stance, 2m)

4 sets × 3 reps/side · 90s rest
WHY: Most squash sprints are lateral. This is the exact first step in every rally.
  • From T-position crouch: split stance
  • Explosive lateral first step — drive opposite hip
  • Stop at 2m — hold position 1s
  • This trains the most common first movement in squash
🧠 Mental — Speed session — embedded

Process focus during sprints: Focus ONLY on your first step during every sprint — not the time, not how you feel, not what's next. Just the first step. This trains the process focus that prevents overthinking under pressure. Metric: Did you think about anything else during the sprint? Aim for No.

SATURDAY Full rest + weekly debrief 😴 Rest ▾

Duration: 0 min  ·  Intensity: Complete recovery  ·  Zero structured training. Light walk only. Eat full macros — do not restrict on rest days. Every adaptation from this week happens TODAY.

🧠 Mental — Saturday evening (20 min)

Weekly review — full debrief: Answer with brutal honesty: (1) Hit every session? If not — why? (2) Are weights going up? (3) Am I better at one specific court thing? (4) Rate mental skills 1–10: focus, confidence, composure, resilience, motivation. (5) Sleep average this week? (6) One thing to do differently. (7) One thing I did well. This review IS training. Metric: Overall mental performance this week 1–10. Trending up from last week?

Week 35 focus: Systematic progression. Log all numbers. What measured improves, what measured stagnates tells you what to fix.

SUNDAY Gym A — Lower body + plyometrics 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–70 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–9  ·  Plyometrics ALWAYS first when fresh. Never under fatigue. Log every weight.

Warm-up

— · 10 min
WHY: Full neural + ankle priming before loading.
  • 5 min easy bike or rower
  • Ankle alphabet: draw A–Z with each foot while seated × 1 each
  • Hip 90/90 mobility × 45s each side
  • Glute bridge × 10 (activate glutes)
  • Dead bug × 6 each side, slow
  • Bodyweight split squat × 5 each side — test ankle response

Lateral bound + stick landingLevel 4 Plyo

4 sets × 4/side · 90s rest
WHY: Level 4 horizontal power + deceleration. The squash push-off in isolation. Stick landing builds deceleration strength directly.
  • Drive explosively off outside foot
  • Land single-leg — stick for 2 full seconds
  • The 2-second hold IS the training — do not cheat it
  • Ankle brace on if any residual instability

Leg press (machine)

4 sets × 6 reps · 2–3 min rest · Tempo: 3-0-X · ~276kg target
WHY: Primary quad builder. 3-second eccentric builds tendon stiffness — more important than the weight right now in block 1. Log weight every session.
  • Full range — all the way down (3s)
  • Feet shoulder-width, toes 10–15° out
  • Drive through heels — not toes
  • Last 2 reps should feel hard (RPE 7–8) after block 1

DB RFESS — Rear foot elevated split squat

4 sets × 4–5 reps/side · 2 min rest · Tempo: 3-1-X · 38–42kg each hand
WHY: Most squash-specific gym exercise. Rear foot elevated = quad + glute loaded in deep lunge position, exactly what every squash shot demands. This exercise never leaves your program.
  • Rear foot on bench (15–25cm height)
  • Front foot far enough forward — front shin vertical at bottom
  • 3s eccentric — own the bottom for 1s — explosive drive up
  • Front heel NEVER lifts
  • Drive back leg into bench for stability
  • Log the DB weight every session — it must go up

Glute bridge (BW or DB on hips)

3 sets × 12 reps · 60s rest
WHY: Hip extension power for every push-off on court. Zero ankle stress.
  • Pause 2s at top — squeeze glutes hard
  • Drive through heels
  • Do not use lower back — glutes only

Lateral lunge (DB)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest · Tempo: 3-2-1
WHY: Squash is laterally dominant. Lateral lunge loads adductors and quads in the wide positions your body takes on court.
  • Step directly sideways — deep lunge
  • 3s into position, 2s hold — feel adductors under load
  • Keep non-lunging leg straight
  • Drive back through mid-foot

Hip thrust (machine or DB on hips)

3 sets × 10 reps · 75s rest
WHY: Glute strength for sprint mechanics and court push-off.
  • Full hip extension at top — pause 1s
  • Ribs down, chin tucked
  • Drive through heels

Seated calf raise (machine or DB on knee)ANKLE

4 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Soleus loading = Achilles protection. Bent knee specifically loads the soleus. Non-negotiable with ankle history — start day 1 and never stop.
  • 3s lowering — this is where the adaptation is
  • Full range up AND down
  • Feel the stretch at the bottom of every rep

Standing calf raise (machine or heavy DB)

4 sets × 10 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Gastrocnemius strength. Standing = ankle in extension = explosive push-off position.
  • 3s lowering
  • Should struggle by rep 8
  • Straight knee throughout

Pallof press (cable or band)

3 sets × 10 reps/side · 45s rest
WHY: Anti-rotation core stiffness. Squash demands both producing AND resisting rotation.
  • Press out — hold 1s — return
  • Zero trunk rotation — that IS the exercise
  • Stand perpendicular to cable/band anchor

Dead bug

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 45s rest · Slow
WHY: Lumbar stabilisation under limb loading. Foundation of all core work.
  • Lower back GLUED to floor — if it lifts, reduce range
  • Opposite arm + leg extend simultaneously
  • Exhale as you extend
🧠 Mental — Before session (5 min)

Box breathing + intention: 5 cycles: inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s. Then say aloud: 'Today I build the foundation of my strength. Every rep is technique.' Start this now — it programs pre-competition composure. Metric: Anxiety 1–10 before session (note in log)

MONDAY RSA — Match simulation 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 50 min  ·  Intensity: Very high  ·  Hardest conditioning session of the week. This IS 5-game fitness training.

Warm-up

12 min
  • Easy ghosting
  • Footwork patterns
  • 3 court build-up sprints

Match-simulation RSA

3 sets × 8–10 reps · 20s rest between reps · 5 min between sets
WHY: RSA training directly trains the ATP-PCr + aerobic interface. Identical sprint quality in rep 10 as rep 1 = game-5 quality maintained.
  • Each rep: full 6-corner ghost at MAXIMUM speed
  • 20s rest = between-point rest in squash — intentionally short
  • Stop a set when slowest rep >8% slower than fastest
  • Log: fastest and slowest rep each set
  • 5 min between sets is mandatory — use the timer

Threshold ghost

2 sets × 4 min on / 90s easy
WHY: Simulates game 3–5 physiological state.
  • RPE 7–8 sustained — not sprinting
  • Count corner touches per minute — log this
  • Same quality on set 2 as set 1 = you are game-5 ready
🧠 Mental — During RSA

Process cue only: During every rep: focus ONLY on the next corner. Not the score, not how tired you are — just the next corner. This is the same present-moment focus used by Farag in game 5 tiebreaks. Metric: Focus breaks per RSA set (target: 0)

TUESDAY Gym B — Upper body + posterior chain + Nordic curls 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–65 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–9  ·  Nordic curls this week: 3×8 reps (5s + quality). Lower as slowly as you can. Non-negotiable.

Warm-up

— · 8 min
WHY: Posterior chain + shoulder activation.
  • 5 min easy rower
  • Band pull-aparts × 15
  • Shoulder ER band × 10 each side
  • Hip hinge drill × 8 (push hips back, feel hamstrings)
  • Single-leg calf raise × 8 each side, slow

DB Romanian Deadlift (RDL)

3 sets × 10 reps · 2 min rest · Tempo: 3-0-1 · 40–45kg+ each hand
WHY: Trains hamstring in lengthened position — exactly when it tears during sprinting and COD. 3s eccentric is where the adaptation happens.
  • Hinge at hip — push hips BACK first, not down
  • Soft bend in knees — this is NOT a squat
  • 3s lowering — feel full hamstring stretch at bottom
  • Neutral spine throughout — do not round lower back
  • Heavier each week — log it

Single-leg RDL (DB balance)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 90s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Proprioception + hamstring under single-leg balance challenge. Mirrors on-court landing mechanics directly.
  • Balance on one leg — soft knee
  • Hinge forward, feel hamstring of standing leg
  • Hold DB on opposite side to standing leg
  • Ankle of standing leg is training too — stay stable

Nordic hamstring curl

3×8 reps (5s + quality) · 90s rest · 4–5 second lowering
WHY: Most evidence-based injury prevention exercise. Reduces hamstring injury 57–70%. Non-negotiable — this is a 16-week progression to 3×8 unassisted.
  • Knees on pad, ankles held by bench or partner
  • Lower AS SLOWLY AS POSSIBLE — 4–5 full seconds
  • Catch at 45° — use hands only as needed
  • Full reset between reps
  • NEVER add reps beyond progression — the DOMS is severe

Pull-up (or assisted machine)

4 sets × 6–8 reps · 2 min rest
WHY: Lat + bicep + scapular control. Pulling volume must exceed pressing — squash swings create chronic internal rotation dominance.
  • Dead hang start — full arm extension
  • Pull chest toward bar (not chin)
  • 2s controlled lowering
  • If 0 pull-ups: assisted machine until you can do 3 unassisted

Cable face pull

3 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Slow
WHY: Posterior deltoid + external rotators. Counteracts chronic internal rotation from squash swings. Shoulder longevity.
  • Pull to NOSE height — not chin, not forehead
  • Elbows flare HIGH and WIDE at end
  • Slow controlled throughout
  • Light weight — this is health work

Landmine press (or kneeling DB press)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest
WHY: Scapula-friendly pressing. Maintains pushing strength without impingement risk.
  • Kneeling = trunk stability challenge
  • Press at 45° angle
  • Do not arch lower back

Cable rotational chop (high → low)

3 sets × 6 reps/side · 60s rest · Light weight, fast concentric
WHY: Rotational power chain — hip-to-trunk force transfer for every squash shot. Mirrors the forehand drive plane.
  • Light-moderate weight — FAST concentric
  • Drive from back hip first
  • Controlled return (3s)
  • Progress weight only when speed stays fast

Soleus ISO (seated, knee bent 90°)ANKLE

3 sets × 35s each side · 30s rest
WHY: Primary Achilles tendon loading position. Gold standard for Achilles prevention with ankle injury history.
  • Knee bent ~90° — this is critical
  • Maximum force into floor
  • Hold 35s — do not reduce effort
  • Progress: bodyweight → DB on knee

Tibialis raise (against wall)ANKLE

2 sets × 15–20 reps · 30s rest
WHY: Tibialis anterior strength. Controls foot position on court. Prevents shin splints.
  • Back flat against wall
  • Lift toes as high as possible
  • Controlled lowering
  • Feel the shin working
🧠 Mental — After session (10 min)

Post-session journal: Write 3 things: (1) What went well physically or mentally. (2) One specific improvement. (3) 'I am building the kind of player who...' sentence. Identity reinforcement builds confidence from evidence, not wishful thinking. Metric: Confidence rating after session (1–10)

WEDNESDAY Court — COD + ghosting + RSA 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 45–50 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–8  ·  Ankle brace mandatory for all court sessions through week 14. Log corner count metric every session.

Movement prep

— · 10 min
  • Ankle prep
  • Technical ghost 5 min at 60%
  • 3 build-up court sprints

Elite COD sequence

3 sets · 45s between sets
WHY: Full COD quality battery. Every rep: braking quality first.
  • Decel to stick × 3 each side
  • 45° cuts × 4 each side
  • 180° T-junction turns × 3
  • Reactive cut on partner cue (or self-call) × 3

Threshold ghost — 5-game simulation

3 sets × 4 min on / 90s easy
WHY: VT2 threshold in squash-specific movement. Trains game 3–5 physiological state.
  • RPE 7–8 sustained — not sprinting
  • Count corner touches per minute
  • Same quality on set 3 as set 1 = game-5 ready
  • Log the corner count — it's your fitness KPI

REACTIVE ghost — partner or self

2 sets × 2 min · 60s rest
WHY: Elite reactive agility under fatigue. Replicates game-5 decision-making.
  • Partner calls corners OR self-call randomly
  • Eyes up — anticipate early
  • Add: wrong cue = don't move (decision quality training)
  • Fast, decisive, accurate
🧠 Mental — During court session

Narrow-wide attention switching: NARROW focus during each sprint to corner. WIDE tactical focus on T return. Practice switching deliberately. This is the attention skill that separates elite from world-class. Metric: Focus quality 1–10 (honest self-rating)

THURSDAY Prehab + Ankle rehab 🏋 Gym🏊 Pool ▾

Duration: 30–35 min  ·  Intensity: Easy  ·  PHASE 4 — Full functional sport loading maintained long-term. Never skip this session — it is what keeps you on court for the other 6 days.

Ankle alphabet — active ROMANKLE

2 sets · A–Z each foot · Slow and deliberate
WHY: Restores full talocrural joint ROM. Grade-3 sprain = scar tissue restriction. Work through every direction.
  • Seated, foot off floor
  • Write A through Z slowly
  • Feel resistance in some directions — work through gently
  • Always first, before any weight-bearing ankle work

Single-leg balanceANKLE

3 sets × 45s · Perturbation (partner pushes) · Sport-specific challenge
WHY: Rebuilds ankle mechanoreceptors destroyed by grade-3 sprain. Balance training reduces ankle re-injury ~46%. Research-backed.
  • Soft knee — never locked
  • Focus on fixed point on wall
  • Progress challenge each week — eyes open is too easy after week 4
  • Grade-3 sprain = destroyed proprioception. You are re-training neural feedback.

Peroneal eccentric (resistance band)ANKLE

3 sets × 12 reps/side · 30s rest · Slow
WHY: Grade-3 sprains damage peroneal tendons — your lateral ankle stabilisers. This rebuilds them eccentrically. Without this, re-injury risk stays elevated indefinitely.
  • Band around front of foot, anchored to the side
  • Evert foot outward against resistance
  • Control the return SLOWLY — that is the eccentric
  • Feel outside of lower leg working

Eccentric calf raise on stepANKLE

3 sets × 12 reps · 45s rest · 3–5s lowering · Single-leg
WHY: Eccentric loading = gold standard for Achilles tendon health. Progressive load from bilateral to single-leg.
  • Rise on TWO feet at top
  • Lower on ONE foot — 3–5s slow descent
  • Full heel drop below step at bottom
  • If ankle pain >2/5: bilateral lowering only

Lateral hop and stickANKLEPhase 3+

3 sets × 5 hops/side · 45s rest · Hold stick 3s
WHY: Phase 3 ankle: dynamic lateral loading. Recreates lateral ankle stress of squash COD.
  • Hop laterally 20–30cm
  • Land SINGLE-LEG — stick for 3 full seconds
  • No ankle wobble = ready to progress
  • If ankle pain >2/5: bilateral hop only

Shoulder prehab circuit

3 rounds · Short rest between exercises
WHY: Rotator cuff health — every squash swing decelerates eccentrically through the cuff.
  • Band ER × 15 each side
  • Face pull × 15
  • Prone Y raise × 10
  • Band pull-apart × 15

Copenhagen plank (groin + adductor)

2 sets × 45s+ each side · Long lever (ankle on bench)
WHY: Adductor strength = documented groin injury prevention. Copenhagen plank is the most specific adductor loading tool.
  • Top leg on bench, bottom leg free
  • Hips SQUARE — do not rotate
  • Short lever first (4–6 weeks), then progress to long lever
  • Progress 5s each week

Hip 90/90 stretch + active reps

2 sets × 45s hold + 8 active reps/side
WHY: Hip rotation mobility needed for lunge depth and shot production.
  • Passive hold first
  • Then: lift front shin, hold 3s, lower × 8
  • Builds strength at end range — not just passive flexibility
🧠 Mental — Throughout prehab session

Intentional breathing: Breathe 4-2-6 (inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s) through EVERY exercise. Prehab becomes a breathing meditation. Lowers cortisol, builds parasympathetic activation — the same state needed for clutch points. Metric: Did breathing feel more natural by the end? Yes/No

FRIDAY Speed — ATP-PCr track session 🏃 Track ▾

Duration: 30–35 min  ·  Intensity: Max on sprint reps  ·  ANKLE BRACE MANDATORY. 3 min rest between EVERY rep — the rest IS the training. This is not conditioning.

Dynamic warm-up

10 min
WHY: Neural priming — cannot sprint well without it.
  • High knees × 20
  • Heel flicks × 20
  • Lateral shuffle 5m × 4
  • Hip openers × 10/side
  • 3 × 20m build-up at 70/85/95%
  • Ankle alphabet × 1 each foot

6-second MAX acceleration

12 reps · 1 rep each · 3 min FULL rest between EVERY rep
WHY: 6s sprint = 94% ATP-PCr energy. 3 min rest = >95% PCr resynthesised. This trains alactic power AND aerobic PCr resynthesis rate = game-5 first-step speed.
  • From squash ready position: crouch, weight forward, feet split
  • Drive back leg through floor — first step is everything
  • MAXIMUM effort — 100%, not 95%
  • Stop at 6 seconds — do not continue
  • 3 min timer between every rep — use sidebar
  • Stop session if any rep feels >10% slower than your best rep

10m acceleration

5 reps · 3–4 min full rest each
WHY: First-step + continuation speed development.
  • Same mechanics — push through to 10m
  • Full explosive push from start
  • Full recovery every rep — same quality rules apply

Lateral first-step (from T-stance, 2m)

4 sets × 3 reps/side · 90s rest
WHY: Most squash sprints are lateral. This is the exact first step in every rally.
  • From T-position crouch: split stance
  • Explosive lateral first step — drive opposite hip
  • Stop at 2m — hold position 1s
  • This trains the most common first movement in squash
🧠 Mental — Speed session — embedded

Process focus during sprints: Focus ONLY on your first step during every sprint — not the time, not how you feel, not what's next. Just the first step. This trains the process focus that prevents overthinking under pressure. Metric: Did you think about anything else during the sprint? Aim for No.

SATURDAY Full rest + weekly debrief 😴 Rest ▾

Duration: 0 min  ·  Intensity: Complete recovery  ·  Zero structured training. Light walk only. Eat full macros — do not restrict on rest days. Every adaptation from this week happens TODAY.

🧠 Mental — Saturday evening (20 min)

Weekly review — full debrief: Answer with brutal honesty: (1) Hit every session? If not — why? (2) Are weights going up? (3) Am I better at one specific court thing? (4) Rate mental skills 1–10: focus, confidence, composure, resilience, motivation. (5) Sleep average this week? (6) One thing to do differently. (7) One thing I did well. This review IS training. Metric: Overall mental performance this week 1–10. Trending up from last week?

Week 36 focus: Systematic progression. Log all numbers. What measured improves, what measured stagnates tells you what to fix.

SUNDAY Gym A — Lower body + plyometrics 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–70 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–9  ·  Plyometrics ALWAYS first when fresh. Never under fatigue. Log every weight.

Warm-up

— · 10 min
WHY: Full neural + ankle priming before loading.
  • 5 min easy bike or rower
  • Ankle alphabet: draw A–Z with each foot while seated × 1 each
  • Hip 90/90 mobility × 45s each side
  • Glute bridge × 10 (activate glutes)
  • Dead bug × 6 each side, slow
  • Bodyweight split squat × 5 each side — test ankle response

Lateral bound + stick landingLevel 4 Plyo

4 sets × 4/side · 90s rest
WHY: Level 4 horizontal power + deceleration. The squash push-off in isolation. Stick landing builds deceleration strength directly.
  • Drive explosively off outside foot
  • Land single-leg — stick for 2 full seconds
  • The 2-second hold IS the training — do not cheat it
  • Ankle brace on if any residual instability

Leg press (machine)

4 sets × 6 reps · 2–3 min rest · Tempo: 3-0-X · ~284kg target
WHY: Primary quad builder. 3-second eccentric builds tendon stiffness — more important than the weight right now in block 1. Log weight every session.
  • Full range — all the way down (3s)
  • Feet shoulder-width, toes 10–15° out
  • Drive through heels — not toes
  • Last 2 reps should feel hard (RPE 7–8) after block 1

DB RFESS — Rear foot elevated split squat

4 sets × 4–5 reps/side · 2 min rest · Tempo: 3-1-X · 38–42kg each hand
WHY: Most squash-specific gym exercise. Rear foot elevated = quad + glute loaded in deep lunge position, exactly what every squash shot demands. This exercise never leaves your program.
  • Rear foot on bench (15–25cm height)
  • Front foot far enough forward — front shin vertical at bottom
  • 3s eccentric — own the bottom for 1s — explosive drive up
  • Front heel NEVER lifts
  • Drive back leg into bench for stability
  • Log the DB weight every session — it must go up

Glute bridge (BW or DB on hips)

3 sets × 12 reps · 60s rest
WHY: Hip extension power for every push-off on court. Zero ankle stress.
  • Pause 2s at top — squeeze glutes hard
  • Drive through heels
  • Do not use lower back — glutes only

Lateral lunge (DB)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest · Tempo: 3-2-1
WHY: Squash is laterally dominant. Lateral lunge loads adductors and quads in the wide positions your body takes on court.
  • Step directly sideways — deep lunge
  • 3s into position, 2s hold — feel adductors under load
  • Keep non-lunging leg straight
  • Drive back through mid-foot

Hip thrust (machine or DB on hips)

3 sets × 10 reps · 75s rest
WHY: Glute strength for sprint mechanics and court push-off.
  • Full hip extension at top — pause 1s
  • Ribs down, chin tucked
  • Drive through heels

Seated calf raise (machine or DB on knee)ANKLE

4 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Soleus loading = Achilles protection. Bent knee specifically loads the soleus. Non-negotiable with ankle history — start day 1 and never stop.
  • 3s lowering — this is where the adaptation is
  • Full range up AND down
  • Feel the stretch at the bottom of every rep

Standing calf raise (machine or heavy DB)

4 sets × 10 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Gastrocnemius strength. Standing = ankle in extension = explosive push-off position.
  • 3s lowering
  • Should struggle by rep 8
  • Straight knee throughout

Pallof press (cable or band)

3 sets × 10 reps/side · 45s rest
WHY: Anti-rotation core stiffness. Squash demands both producing AND resisting rotation.
  • Press out — hold 1s — return
  • Zero trunk rotation — that IS the exercise
  • Stand perpendicular to cable/band anchor

Dead bug

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 45s rest · Slow
WHY: Lumbar stabilisation under limb loading. Foundation of all core work.
  • Lower back GLUED to floor — if it lifts, reduce range
  • Opposite arm + leg extend simultaneously
  • Exhale as you extend
🧠 Mental — Before session (5 min)

Box breathing + intention: 5 cycles: inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s. Then say aloud: 'Today I build the foundation of my strength. Every rep is technique.' Start this now — it programs pre-competition composure. Metric: Anxiety 1–10 before session (note in log)

MONDAY RSA — Match simulation 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 50 min  ·  Intensity: Very high  ·  Hardest conditioning session of the week. This IS 5-game fitness training.

Warm-up

12 min
  • Easy ghosting
  • Footwork patterns
  • 3 court build-up sprints

Match-simulation RSA

3 sets × 8–10 reps · 20s rest between reps · 5 min between sets
WHY: RSA training directly trains the ATP-PCr + aerobic interface. Identical sprint quality in rep 10 as rep 1 = game-5 quality maintained.
  • Each rep: full 6-corner ghost at MAXIMUM speed
  • 20s rest = between-point rest in squash — intentionally short
  • Stop a set when slowest rep >8% slower than fastest
  • Log: fastest and slowest rep each set
  • 5 min between sets is mandatory — use the timer

Threshold ghost

2 sets × 4 min on / 90s easy
WHY: Simulates game 3–5 physiological state.
  • RPE 7–8 sustained — not sprinting
  • Count corner touches per minute — log this
  • Same quality on set 2 as set 1 = you are game-5 ready
🧠 Mental — During RSA

Process cue only: During every rep: focus ONLY on the next corner. Not the score, not how tired you are — just the next corner. This is the same present-moment focus used by Farag in game 5 tiebreaks. Metric: Focus breaks per RSA set (target: 0)

TUESDAY Gym B — Upper body + posterior chain + Nordic curls 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–65 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–9  ·  Nordic curls this week: 3×8 reps (5s + quality). Lower as slowly as you can. Non-negotiable.

Warm-up

— · 8 min
WHY: Posterior chain + shoulder activation.
  • 5 min easy rower
  • Band pull-aparts × 15
  • Shoulder ER band × 10 each side
  • Hip hinge drill × 8 (push hips back, feel hamstrings)
  • Single-leg calf raise × 8 each side, slow

DB Romanian Deadlift (RDL)

3 sets × 10 reps · 2 min rest · Tempo: 3-0-1 · 40–45kg+ each hand
WHY: Trains hamstring in lengthened position — exactly when it tears during sprinting and COD. 3s eccentric is where the adaptation happens.
  • Hinge at hip — push hips BACK first, not down
  • Soft bend in knees — this is NOT a squat
  • 3s lowering — feel full hamstring stretch at bottom
  • Neutral spine throughout — do not round lower back
  • Heavier each week — log it

Single-leg RDL (DB balance)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 90s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Proprioception + hamstring under single-leg balance challenge. Mirrors on-court landing mechanics directly.
  • Balance on one leg — soft knee
  • Hinge forward, feel hamstring of standing leg
  • Hold DB on opposite side to standing leg
  • Ankle of standing leg is training too — stay stable

Nordic hamstring curl

3×8 reps (5s + quality) · 90s rest · 4–5 second lowering
WHY: Most evidence-based injury prevention exercise. Reduces hamstring injury 57–70%. Non-negotiable — this is a 16-week progression to 3×8 unassisted.
  • Knees on pad, ankles held by bench or partner
  • Lower AS SLOWLY AS POSSIBLE — 4–5 full seconds
  • Catch at 45° — use hands only as needed
  • Full reset between reps
  • NEVER add reps beyond progression — the DOMS is severe

Pull-up (or assisted machine)

4 sets × 6–8 reps · 2 min rest
WHY: Lat + bicep + scapular control. Pulling volume must exceed pressing — squash swings create chronic internal rotation dominance.
  • Dead hang start — full arm extension
  • Pull chest toward bar (not chin)
  • 2s controlled lowering
  • If 0 pull-ups: assisted machine until you can do 3 unassisted

Cable face pull

3 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Slow
WHY: Posterior deltoid + external rotators. Counteracts chronic internal rotation from squash swings. Shoulder longevity.
  • Pull to NOSE height — not chin, not forehead
  • Elbows flare HIGH and WIDE at end
  • Slow controlled throughout
  • Light weight — this is health work

Landmine press (or kneeling DB press)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest
WHY: Scapula-friendly pressing. Maintains pushing strength without impingement risk.
  • Kneeling = trunk stability challenge
  • Press at 45° angle
  • Do not arch lower back

Cable rotational chop (high → low)

3 sets × 6 reps/side · 60s rest · Light weight, fast concentric
WHY: Rotational power chain — hip-to-trunk force transfer for every squash shot. Mirrors the forehand drive plane.
  • Light-moderate weight — FAST concentric
  • Drive from back hip first
  • Controlled return (3s)
  • Progress weight only when speed stays fast

Soleus ISO (seated, knee bent 90°)ANKLE

3 sets × 35s each side · 30s rest
WHY: Primary Achilles tendon loading position. Gold standard for Achilles prevention with ankle injury history.
  • Knee bent ~90° — this is critical
  • Maximum force into floor
  • Hold 35s — do not reduce effort
  • Progress: bodyweight → DB on knee

Tibialis raise (against wall)ANKLE

2 sets × 15–20 reps · 30s rest
WHY: Tibialis anterior strength. Controls foot position on court. Prevents shin splints.
  • Back flat against wall
  • Lift toes as high as possible
  • Controlled lowering
  • Feel the shin working
🧠 Mental — After session (10 min)

Post-session journal: Write 3 things: (1) What went well physically or mentally. (2) One specific improvement. (3) 'I am building the kind of player who...' sentence. Identity reinforcement builds confidence from evidence, not wishful thinking. Metric: Confidence rating after session (1–10)

WEDNESDAY Court — COD + ghosting + RSA 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 45–50 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–8  ·  Ankle brace mandatory for all court sessions through week 14. Log corner count metric every session.

Movement prep

— · 10 min
  • Ankle prep
  • Technical ghost 5 min at 60%
  • 3 build-up court sprints

Elite COD sequence

3 sets · 45s between sets
WHY: Full COD quality battery. Every rep: braking quality first.
  • Decel to stick × 3 each side
  • 45° cuts × 4 each side
  • 180° T-junction turns × 3
  • Reactive cut on partner cue (or self-call) × 3

Threshold ghost — 5-game simulation

3 sets × 4 min on / 90s easy
WHY: VT2 threshold in squash-specific movement. Trains game 3–5 physiological state.
  • RPE 7–8 sustained — not sprinting
  • Count corner touches per minute
  • Same quality on set 3 as set 1 = game-5 ready
  • Log the corner count — it's your fitness KPI

REACTIVE ghost — partner or self

2 sets × 2 min · 60s rest
WHY: Elite reactive agility under fatigue. Replicates game-5 decision-making.
  • Partner calls corners OR self-call randomly
  • Eyes up — anticipate early
  • Add: wrong cue = don't move (decision quality training)
  • Fast, decisive, accurate
🧠 Mental — During court session

Narrow-wide attention switching: NARROW focus during each sprint to corner. WIDE tactical focus on T return. Practice switching deliberately. This is the attention skill that separates elite from world-class. Metric: Focus quality 1–10 (honest self-rating)

THURSDAY Prehab + Ankle rehab 🏋 Gym🏊 Pool ▾

Duration: 30–35 min  ·  Intensity: Easy  ·  PHASE 4 — Full functional sport loading maintained long-term. Never skip this session — it is what keeps you on court for the other 6 days.

Ankle alphabet — active ROMANKLE

2 sets · A–Z each foot · Slow and deliberate
WHY: Restores full talocrural joint ROM. Grade-3 sprain = scar tissue restriction. Work through every direction.
  • Seated, foot off floor
  • Write A through Z slowly
  • Feel resistance in some directions — work through gently
  • Always first, before any weight-bearing ankle work

Single-leg balanceANKLE

3 sets × 45s · Perturbation (partner pushes) · Sport-specific challenge
WHY: Rebuilds ankle mechanoreceptors destroyed by grade-3 sprain. Balance training reduces ankle re-injury ~46%. Research-backed.
  • Soft knee — never locked
  • Focus on fixed point on wall
  • Progress challenge each week — eyes open is too easy after week 4
  • Grade-3 sprain = destroyed proprioception. You are re-training neural feedback.

Peroneal eccentric (resistance band)ANKLE

3 sets × 12 reps/side · 30s rest · Slow
WHY: Grade-3 sprains damage peroneal tendons — your lateral ankle stabilisers. This rebuilds them eccentrically. Without this, re-injury risk stays elevated indefinitely.
  • Band around front of foot, anchored to the side
  • Evert foot outward against resistance
  • Control the return SLOWLY — that is the eccentric
  • Feel outside of lower leg working

Eccentric calf raise on stepANKLE

3 sets × 12 reps · 45s rest · 3–5s lowering · Single-leg
WHY: Eccentric loading = gold standard for Achilles tendon health. Progressive load from bilateral to single-leg.
  • Rise on TWO feet at top
  • Lower on ONE foot — 3–5s slow descent
  • Full heel drop below step at bottom
  • If ankle pain >2/5: bilateral lowering only

Lateral hop and stickANKLEPhase 3+

3 sets × 5 hops/side · 45s rest · Hold stick 3s
WHY: Phase 3 ankle: dynamic lateral loading. Recreates lateral ankle stress of squash COD.
  • Hop laterally 20–30cm
  • Land SINGLE-LEG — stick for 3 full seconds
  • No ankle wobble = ready to progress
  • If ankle pain >2/5: bilateral hop only

Shoulder prehab circuit

3 rounds · Short rest between exercises
WHY: Rotator cuff health — every squash swing decelerates eccentrically through the cuff.
  • Band ER × 15 each side
  • Face pull × 15
  • Prone Y raise × 10
  • Band pull-apart × 15

Copenhagen plank (groin + adductor)

2 sets × 45s+ each side · Long lever (ankle on bench)
WHY: Adductor strength = documented groin injury prevention. Copenhagen plank is the most specific adductor loading tool.
  • Top leg on bench, bottom leg free
  • Hips SQUARE — do not rotate
  • Short lever first (4–6 weeks), then progress to long lever
  • Progress 5s each week

Hip 90/90 stretch + active reps

2 sets × 45s hold + 8 active reps/side
WHY: Hip rotation mobility needed for lunge depth and shot production.
  • Passive hold first
  • Then: lift front shin, hold 3s, lower × 8
  • Builds strength at end range — not just passive flexibility
🧠 Mental — Throughout prehab session

Intentional breathing: Breathe 4-2-6 (inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s) through EVERY exercise. Prehab becomes a breathing meditation. Lowers cortisol, builds parasympathetic activation — the same state needed for clutch points. Metric: Did breathing feel more natural by the end? Yes/No

FRIDAY Speed — ATP-PCr track session 🏃 Track ▾

Duration: 30–35 min  ·  Intensity: Max on sprint reps  ·  ANKLE BRACE MANDATORY. 3 min rest between EVERY rep — the rest IS the training. This is not conditioning.

Dynamic warm-up

10 min
WHY: Neural priming — cannot sprint well without it.
  • High knees × 20
  • Heel flicks × 20
  • Lateral shuffle 5m × 4
  • Hip openers × 10/side
  • 3 × 20m build-up at 70/85/95%
  • Ankle alphabet × 1 each foot

6-second MAX acceleration

12 reps · 1 rep each · 3 min FULL rest between EVERY rep
WHY: 6s sprint = 94% ATP-PCr energy. 3 min rest = >95% PCr resynthesised. This trains alactic power AND aerobic PCr resynthesis rate = game-5 first-step speed.
  • From squash ready position: crouch, weight forward, feet split
  • Drive back leg through floor — first step is everything
  • MAXIMUM effort — 100%, not 95%
  • Stop at 6 seconds — do not continue
  • 3 min timer between every rep — use sidebar
  • Stop session if any rep feels >10% slower than your best rep

10m acceleration

5 reps · 3–4 min full rest each
WHY: First-step + continuation speed development.
  • Same mechanics — push through to 10m
  • Full explosive push from start
  • Full recovery every rep — same quality rules apply

Lateral first-step (from T-stance, 2m)

4 sets × 3 reps/side · 90s rest
WHY: Most squash sprints are lateral. This is the exact first step in every rally.
  • From T-position crouch: split stance
  • Explosive lateral first step — drive opposite hip
  • Stop at 2m — hold position 1s
  • This trains the most common first movement in squash
🧠 Mental — Speed session — embedded

Process focus during sprints: Focus ONLY on your first step during every sprint — not the time, not how you feel, not what's next. Just the first step. This trains the process focus that prevents overthinking under pressure. Metric: Did you think about anything else during the sprint? Aim for No.

SATURDAY Full rest + weekly debrief 😴 Rest ▾

Duration: 0 min  ·  Intensity: Complete recovery  ·  Zero structured training. Light walk only. Eat full macros — do not restrict on rest days. Every adaptation from this week happens TODAY.

🧠 Mental — Saturday evening (20 min)

Weekly review — full debrief: Answer with brutal honesty: (1) Hit every session? If not — why? (2) Are weights going up? (3) Am I better at one specific court thing? (4) Rate mental skills 1–10: focus, confidence, composure, resilience, motivation. (5) Sleep average this week? (6) One thing to do differently. (7) One thing I did well. This review IS training. Metric: Overall mental performance this week 1–10. Trending up from last week?

Week 37 focus: Systematic progression. Log all numbers. What measured improves, what measured stagnates tells you what to fix.

SUNDAY Gym A — Lower body + plyometrics 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–70 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 6–7  ·  Plyometrics ALWAYS first when fresh. Never under fatigue. Log every weight.

Warm-up

— · 10 min
WHY: Full neural + ankle priming before loading.
  • 5 min easy bike or rower
  • Ankle alphabet: draw A–Z with each foot while seated × 1 each
  • Hip 90/90 mobility × 45s each side
  • Glute bridge × 10 (activate glutes)
  • Dead bug × 6 each side, slow
  • Bodyweight split squat × 5 each side — test ankle response

Lateral bound + stick landingLevel 4 Plyo

4 sets × 4/side · 90s rest
WHY: Level 4 horizontal power + deceleration. The squash push-off in isolation. Stick landing builds deceleration strength directly.
  • Drive explosively off outside foot
  • Land single-leg — stick for 2 full seconds
  • The 2-second hold IS the training — do not cheat it
  • Ankle brace on if any residual instability

Leg press (machine)

2 sets × 8 reps · 90s rest · Tempo: 3-0-X · ~292kg target
WHY: Primary quad builder. 3-second eccentric builds tendon stiffness — more important than the weight right now in block 1. Log weight every session.
  • Full range — all the way down (3s)
  • Feet shoulder-width, toes 10–15° out
  • Drive through heels — not toes
  • Last 2 reps should feel hard (RPE 7–8) after block 1

DB RFESS — Rear foot elevated split squat

2 sets × 4–5 reps/side · 90s rest · Tempo: 3-1-X · 38–42kg each hand
WHY: Most squash-specific gym exercise. Rear foot elevated = quad + glute loaded in deep lunge position, exactly what every squash shot demands. This exercise never leaves your program.
  • Rear foot on bench (15–25cm height)
  • Front foot far enough forward — front shin vertical at bottom
  • 3s eccentric — own the bottom for 1s — explosive drive up
  • Front heel NEVER lifts
  • Drive back leg into bench for stability
  • Log the DB weight every session — it must go up

Glute bridge (BW or DB on hips)

3 sets × 12 reps · 60s rest
WHY: Hip extension power for every push-off on court. Zero ankle stress.
  • Pause 2s at top — squeeze glutes hard
  • Drive through heels
  • Do not use lower back — glutes only

Lateral lunge (DB)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest · Tempo: 3-2-1
WHY: Squash is laterally dominant. Lateral lunge loads adductors and quads in the wide positions your body takes on court.
  • Step directly sideways — deep lunge
  • 3s into position, 2s hold — feel adductors under load
  • Keep non-lunging leg straight
  • Drive back through mid-foot

Hip thrust (machine or DB on hips)

3 sets × 10 reps · 75s rest
WHY: Glute strength for sprint mechanics and court push-off.
  • Full hip extension at top — pause 1s
  • Ribs down, chin tucked
  • Drive through heels

Seated calf raise (machine or DB on knee)ANKLE

2 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Soleus loading = Achilles protection. Bent knee specifically loads the soleus. Non-negotiable with ankle history — start day 1 and never stop.
  • 3s lowering — this is where the adaptation is
  • Full range up AND down
  • Feel the stretch at the bottom of every rep

Standing calf raise (machine or heavy DB)

4 sets × 10 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Gastrocnemius strength. Standing = ankle in extension = explosive push-off position.
  • 3s lowering
  • Should struggle by rep 8
  • Straight knee throughout

Pallof press (cable or band)

3 sets × 10 reps/side · 45s rest
WHY: Anti-rotation core stiffness. Squash demands both producing AND resisting rotation.
  • Press out — hold 1s — return
  • Zero trunk rotation — that IS the exercise
  • Stand perpendicular to cable/band anchor

Dead bug

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 45s rest · Slow
WHY: Lumbar stabilisation under limb loading. Foundation of all core work.
  • Lower back GLUED to floor — if it lifts, reduce range
  • Opposite arm + leg extend simultaneously
  • Exhale as you extend
🧠 Mental — Before session (5 min)

Box breathing + intention: 5 cycles: inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s. Then say aloud: 'Today I build the foundation of my strength. Every rep is technique.' Start this now — it programs pre-competition composure. Metric: Anxiety 1–10 before session (note in log)

MONDAY Z2 recovery 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 30 min  ·  Intensity: Easy  ·  Deload: Z2 only.

Easy Z2 pool swim or jog

30 min
WHY: Active recovery.
  • RPE 3–4
  • Nose-breathing
🧠 Mental — During RSA

Process cue only: During every rep: focus ONLY on the next corner. Not the score, not how tired you are — just the next corner. This is the same present-moment focus used by Farag in game 5 tiebreaks. Metric: Focus breaks per RSA set (target: 0)

TUESDAY Gym B — Upper body + posterior chain + Nordic curls 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–65 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 6–7  ·  Nordic curls this week: 3×8 reps (5s + quality). Lower as slowly as you can. Non-negotiable.

Warm-up

— · 8 min
WHY: Posterior chain + shoulder activation.
  • 5 min easy rower
  • Band pull-aparts × 15
  • Shoulder ER band × 10 each side
  • Hip hinge drill × 8 (push hips back, feel hamstrings)
  • Single-leg calf raise × 8 each side, slow

DB Romanian Deadlift (RDL)

2 sets × 10 reps · 90s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1 · 40–45kg+ each hand
WHY: Trains hamstring in lengthened position — exactly when it tears during sprinting and COD. 3s eccentric is where the adaptation happens.
  • Hinge at hip — push hips BACK first, not down
  • Soft bend in knees — this is NOT a squat
  • 3s lowering — feel full hamstring stretch at bottom
  • Neutral spine throughout — do not round lower back
  • Heavier each week — log it

Single-leg RDL (DB balance)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 90s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Proprioception + hamstring under single-leg balance challenge. Mirrors on-court landing mechanics directly.
  • Balance on one leg — soft knee
  • Hinge forward, feel hamstring of standing leg
  • Hold DB on opposite side to standing leg
  • Ankle of standing leg is training too — stay stable

Nordic hamstring curl — SKIP THIS WEEK (deload)

— (deload week)
WHY: Most evidence-based injury prevention exercise. Reduces hamstring injury 57–70%. Non-negotiable — this is a 16-week progression to 3×8 unassisted.
  • Skipped this week — deload protocol

Pull-up (or assisted machine)

2 sets × 6–8 reps · 90s rest
WHY: Lat + bicep + scapular control. Pulling volume must exceed pressing — squash swings create chronic internal rotation dominance.
  • Dead hang start — full arm extension
  • Pull chest toward bar (not chin)
  • 2s controlled lowering
  • If 0 pull-ups: assisted machine until you can do 3 unassisted

Cable face pull

3 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Slow
WHY: Posterior deltoid + external rotators. Counteracts chronic internal rotation from squash swings. Shoulder longevity.
  • Pull to NOSE height — not chin, not forehead
  • Elbows flare HIGH and WIDE at end
  • Slow controlled throughout
  • Light weight — this is health work

Landmine press (or kneeling DB press)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest
WHY: Scapula-friendly pressing. Maintains pushing strength without impingement risk.
  • Kneeling = trunk stability challenge
  • Press at 45° angle
  • Do not arch lower back

Cable rotational chop (high → low)

3 sets × 6 reps/side · 60s rest · Light weight, fast concentric
WHY: Rotational power chain — hip-to-trunk force transfer for every squash shot. Mirrors the forehand drive plane.
  • Light-moderate weight — FAST concentric
  • Drive from back hip first
  • Controlled return (3s)
  • Progress weight only when speed stays fast

Soleus ISO (seated, knee bent 90°)ANKLE

3 sets × 35s each side · 30s rest
WHY: Primary Achilles tendon loading position. Gold standard for Achilles prevention with ankle injury history.
  • Knee bent ~90° — this is critical
  • Maximum force into floor
  • Hold 35s — do not reduce effort
  • Progress: bodyweight → DB on knee

Tibialis raise (against wall)ANKLE

2 sets × 15–20 reps · 30s rest
WHY: Tibialis anterior strength. Controls foot position on court. Prevents shin splints.
  • Back flat against wall
  • Lift toes as high as possible
  • Controlled lowering
  • Feel the shin working
🧠 Mental — After session (10 min)

Post-session journal: Write 3 things: (1) What went well physically or mentally. (2) One specific improvement. (3) 'I am building the kind of player who...' sentence. Identity reinforcement builds confidence from evidence, not wishful thinking. Metric: Confidence rating after session (1–10)

WEDNESDAY Court — COD + ghosting + RSA 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 45–50 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 5–6  ·  Ankle brace mandatory for all court sessions through week 14. Log corner count metric every session.

Movement prep

— · 10 min
  • Ankle prep
  • Technical ghost 5 min at 60%
  • 3 build-up court sprints

Elite COD sequence

3 sets · 45s between sets
WHY: Full COD quality battery. Every rep: braking quality first.
  • Decel to stick × 3 each side
  • 45° cuts × 4 each side
  • 180° T-junction turns × 3
  • Reactive cut on partner cue (or self-call) × 3

Threshold ghost — 5-game simulation

3 sets × 4 min on / 90s easy
WHY: VT2 threshold in squash-specific movement. Trains game 3–5 physiological state.
  • RPE 7–8 sustained — not sprinting
  • Count corner touches per minute
  • Same quality on set 3 as set 1 = game-5 ready
  • Log the corner count — it's your fitness KPI

REACTIVE ghost — partner or self

2 sets × 2 min · 60s rest
WHY: Elite reactive agility under fatigue. Replicates game-5 decision-making.
  • Partner calls corners OR self-call randomly
  • Eyes up — anticipate early
  • Add: wrong cue = don't move (decision quality training)
  • Fast, decisive, accurate
🧠 Mental — During court session

Narrow-wide attention switching: NARROW focus during each sprint to corner. WIDE tactical focus on T return. Practice switching deliberately. This is the attention skill that separates elite from world-class. Metric: Focus quality 1–10 (honest self-rating)

THURSDAY Prehab + Ankle rehab 🏋 Gym🏊 Pool ▾

Duration: 30–35 min  ·  Intensity: Easy  ·  PHASE 4 — Full functional sport loading maintained long-term. Never skip this session — it is what keeps you on court for the other 6 days.

Ankle alphabet — active ROMANKLE

2 sets · A–Z each foot · Slow and deliberate
WHY: Restores full talocrural joint ROM. Grade-3 sprain = scar tissue restriction. Work through every direction.
  • Seated, foot off floor
  • Write A through Z slowly
  • Feel resistance in some directions — work through gently
  • Always first, before any weight-bearing ankle work

Single-leg balanceANKLE

2 sets × 45s · Perturbation (partner pushes) · Sport-specific challenge
WHY: Rebuilds ankle mechanoreceptors destroyed by grade-3 sprain. Balance training reduces ankle re-injury ~46%. Research-backed.
  • Soft knee — never locked
  • Focus on fixed point on wall
  • Progress challenge each week — eyes open is too easy after week 4
  • Grade-3 sprain = destroyed proprioception. You are re-training neural feedback.

Peroneal eccentric (resistance band)ANKLE

3 sets × 12 reps/side · 30s rest · Slow
WHY: Grade-3 sprains damage peroneal tendons — your lateral ankle stabilisers. This rebuilds them eccentrically. Without this, re-injury risk stays elevated indefinitely.
  • Band around front of foot, anchored to the side
  • Evert foot outward against resistance
  • Control the return SLOWLY — that is the eccentric
  • Feel outside of lower leg working

Eccentric calf raise on stepANKLE

2 sets × 12 reps · 45s rest · 3–5s lowering · Single-leg
WHY: Eccentric loading = gold standard for Achilles tendon health. Progressive load from bilateral to single-leg.
  • Rise on TWO feet at top
  • Lower on ONE foot — 3–5s slow descent
  • Full heel drop below step at bottom
  • If ankle pain >2/5: bilateral lowering only

Lateral hop and stickANKLEPhase 3+

3 sets × 5 hops/side · 45s rest · Hold stick 3s
WHY: Phase 3 ankle: dynamic lateral loading. Recreates lateral ankle stress of squash COD.
  • Hop laterally 20–30cm
  • Land SINGLE-LEG — stick for 3 full seconds
  • No ankle wobble = ready to progress
  • If ankle pain >2/5: bilateral hop only

Shoulder prehab circuit

2 rounds · Short rest between exercises
WHY: Rotator cuff health — every squash swing decelerates eccentrically through the cuff.
  • Band ER × 15 each side
  • Face pull × 15
  • Prone Y raise × 10
  • Band pull-apart × 15

Copenhagen plank (groin + adductor)

2 sets × 45s+ each side · Long lever (ankle on bench)
WHY: Adductor strength = documented groin injury prevention. Copenhagen plank is the most specific adductor loading tool.
  • Top leg on bench, bottom leg free
  • Hips SQUARE — do not rotate
  • Short lever first (4–6 weeks), then progress to long lever
  • Progress 5s each week

Hip 90/90 stretch + active reps

2 sets × 45s hold + 8 active reps/side
WHY: Hip rotation mobility needed for lunge depth and shot production.
  • Passive hold first
  • Then: lift front shin, hold 3s, lower × 8
  • Builds strength at end range — not just passive flexibility
🧠 Mental — Throughout prehab session

Intentional breathing: Breathe 4-2-6 (inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s) through EVERY exercise. Prehab becomes a breathing meditation. Lowers cortisol, builds parasympathetic activation — the same state needed for clutch points. Metric: Did breathing feel more natural by the end? Yes/No

FRIDAY Speed — ATP-PCr track session 🏃 Track ▾

Duration: 30–35 min  ·  Intensity: Max on sprint reps  ·  ANKLE BRACE MANDATORY. 3 min rest between EVERY rep — the rest IS the training. This is not conditioning.

Dynamic warm-up

10 min
WHY: Neural priming — cannot sprint well without it.
  • High knees × 20
  • Heel flicks × 20
  • Lateral shuffle 5m × 4
  • Hip openers × 10/side
  • 3 × 20m build-up at 70/85/95%
  • Ankle alphabet × 1 each foot

6-second MAX acceleration

4 reps · 1 rep each · 3 min FULL rest between EVERY rep
WHY: 6s sprint = 94% ATP-PCr energy. 3 min rest = >95% PCr resynthesised. This trains alactic power AND aerobic PCr resynthesis rate = game-5 first-step speed.
  • From squash ready position: crouch, weight forward, feet split
  • Drive back leg through floor — first step is everything
  • MAXIMUM effort — 100%, not 95%
  • Stop at 6 seconds — do not continue
  • 3 min timer between every rep — use sidebar
  • Stop session if any rep feels >10% slower than your best rep

10m acceleration

2 reps · 3–4 min full rest each
WHY: First-step + continuation speed development.
  • Same mechanics — push through to 10m
  • Full explosive push from start
  • Full recovery every rep — same quality rules apply

Lateral first-step (from T-stance, 2m)

2 sets × 3 reps/side · 90s rest
WHY: Most squash sprints are lateral. This is the exact first step in every rally.
  • From T-position crouch: split stance
  • Explosive lateral first step — drive opposite hip
  • Stop at 2m — hold position 1s
  • This trains the most common first movement in squash
🧠 Mental — Speed session — embedded

Process focus during sprints: Focus ONLY on your first step during every sprint — not the time, not how you feel, not what's next. Just the first step. This trains the process focus that prevents overthinking under pressure. Metric: Did you think about anything else during the sprint? Aim for No.

SATURDAY Full rest + weekly debrief 😴 Rest ▾

Duration: 0 min  ·  Intensity: Complete recovery  ·  Zero structured training. Light walk only. Eat full macros — do not restrict on rest days. Every adaptation from this week happens TODAY.

🧠 Mental — Saturday evening (20 min)

Weekly review — full debrief: Answer with brutal honesty: (1) Hit every session? If not — why? (2) Are weights going up? (3) Am I better at one specific court thing? (4) Rate mental skills 1–10: focus, confidence, composure, resilience, motivation. (5) Sleep average this week? (6) One thing to do differently. (7) One thing I did well. This review IS training. Metric: Overall mental performance this week 1–10. Trending up from last week?

10
Phase 4 · Month 10

MONTH 10 — ELITE STANDARD

Weeks 38–41. Physical benchmarks entering elite ranges. RSA approaching sub 221s. 5m sprint approaching sub 0.93s. Tactical sophistication reaching serious competitive level. Mental game consistent under pressure. You are beginning to look and perform like a real squash athlete.

Month goals
  • RSA 10 reps sub 225s, fatigue index <6%
  • 5m sprint sub 0.95s
  • CMJ 35–40cm
  • Threshold ghost: 8+ corners per minute
  • Tactical deception working under match pressure
  • Opponent reading from racquet face (not ball)
New this month
  • RSA at near-elite benchmark
  • Deceptive shot working under real match conditions
  • Opponent reading from racquet face practised
  • Tempo manipulation (volley + lob) tactical awareness
  • HRV monitoring established for daily readiness
  • Weekly mental debrief: all 5 qualities rated
Study assignment
  • Study Mostafa Asal vs Ali Farag — the physical vs tactical contrast. Two different styles at world No.1 standard.
  • Video analysis your last 3 matches: find your dominant shot pattern AND your opponent's most successful counter. Fix the vulnerability.
  • Research: What does the next level of competition require? What tournaments are accessible to you now?
  • Study: volley drop shot execution. This is a game-changer at competitive level. Learn it now.

Week 38 focus: Systematic progression. Log all numbers. What measured improves, what measured stagnates tells you what to fix.

SUNDAY Gym A — Lower body + plyometrics 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–70 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–9  ·  Plyometrics ALWAYS first when fresh. Never under fatigue. Log every weight.

Warm-up

— · 10 min
WHY: Full neural + ankle priming before loading.
  • 5 min easy bike or rower
  • Ankle alphabet: draw A–Z with each foot while seated × 1 each
  • Hip 90/90 mobility × 45s each side
  • Glute bridge × 10 (activate glutes)
  • Dead bug × 6 each side, slow
  • Bodyweight split squat × 5 each side — test ankle response

Lateral bound + stick landingLevel 4 Plyo

4 sets × 4/side · 90s rest
WHY: Level 4 horizontal power + deceleration. The squash push-off in isolation. Stick landing builds deceleration strength directly.
  • Drive explosively off outside foot
  • Land single-leg — stick for 2 full seconds
  • The 2-second hold IS the training — do not cheat it
  • Ankle brace on if any residual instability

Leg press (machine)

4 sets × 6 reps · 2–3 min rest · Tempo: 3-0-X · ~300kg target
WHY: Primary quad builder. 3-second eccentric builds tendon stiffness — more important than the weight right now in block 1. Log weight every session.
  • Full range — all the way down (3s)
  • Feet shoulder-width, toes 10–15° out
  • Drive through heels — not toes
  • Last 2 reps should feel hard (RPE 7–8) after block 1

DB RFESS — Rear foot elevated split squat

4 sets × 4–5 reps/side · 2 min rest · Tempo: 3-1-X · 38–42kg each hand
WHY: Most squash-specific gym exercise. Rear foot elevated = quad + glute loaded in deep lunge position, exactly what every squash shot demands. This exercise never leaves your program.
  • Rear foot on bench (15–25cm height)
  • Front foot far enough forward — front shin vertical at bottom
  • 3s eccentric — own the bottom for 1s — explosive drive up
  • Front heel NEVER lifts
  • Drive back leg into bench for stability
  • Log the DB weight every session — it must go up

Glute bridge (BW or DB on hips)

3 sets × 12 reps · 60s rest
WHY: Hip extension power for every push-off on court. Zero ankle stress.
  • Pause 2s at top — squeeze glutes hard
  • Drive through heels
  • Do not use lower back — glutes only

Lateral lunge (DB)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest · Tempo: 3-2-1
WHY: Squash is laterally dominant. Lateral lunge loads adductors and quads in the wide positions your body takes on court.
  • Step directly sideways — deep lunge
  • 3s into position, 2s hold — feel adductors under load
  • Keep non-lunging leg straight
  • Drive back through mid-foot

Hip thrust (machine or DB on hips)

3 sets × 10 reps · 75s rest
WHY: Glute strength for sprint mechanics and court push-off.
  • Full hip extension at top — pause 1s
  • Ribs down, chin tucked
  • Drive through heels

Seated calf raise (machine or DB on knee)ANKLE

4 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Soleus loading = Achilles protection. Bent knee specifically loads the soleus. Non-negotiable with ankle history — start day 1 and never stop.
  • 3s lowering — this is where the adaptation is
  • Full range up AND down
  • Feel the stretch at the bottom of every rep

Standing calf raise (machine or heavy DB)

4 sets × 10 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Gastrocnemius strength. Standing = ankle in extension = explosive push-off position.
  • 3s lowering
  • Should struggle by rep 8
  • Straight knee throughout

Pallof press (cable or band)

3 sets × 10 reps/side · 45s rest
WHY: Anti-rotation core stiffness. Squash demands both producing AND resisting rotation.
  • Press out — hold 1s — return
  • Zero trunk rotation — that IS the exercise
  • Stand perpendicular to cable/band anchor

Dead bug

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 45s rest · Slow
WHY: Lumbar stabilisation under limb loading. Foundation of all core work.
  • Lower back GLUED to floor — if it lifts, reduce range
  • Opposite arm + leg extend simultaneously
  • Exhale as you extend
🧠 Mental — Before session (5 min)

Box breathing + intention: 5 cycles: inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s. Then say aloud: 'Today I build the foundation of my strength. Every rep is technique.' Start this now — it programs pre-competition composure. Metric: Anxiety 1–10 before session (note in log)

MONDAY RSA — Match simulation 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 50 min  ·  Intensity: Very high  ·  Hardest conditioning session of the week. This IS 5-game fitness training.

Warm-up

12 min
  • Easy ghosting
  • Footwork patterns
  • 3 court build-up sprints

Match-simulation RSA

3 sets × 8–10 reps · 20s rest between reps · 5 min between sets
WHY: RSA training directly trains the ATP-PCr + aerobic interface. Identical sprint quality in rep 10 as rep 1 = game-5 quality maintained.
  • Each rep: full 6-corner ghost at MAXIMUM speed
  • 20s rest = between-point rest in squash — intentionally short
  • Stop a set when slowest rep >8% slower than fastest
  • Log: fastest and slowest rep each set
  • 5 min between sets is mandatory — use the timer

Threshold ghost

2 sets × 4 min on / 90s easy
WHY: Simulates game 3–5 physiological state.
  • RPE 7–8 sustained — not sprinting
  • Count corner touches per minute — log this
  • Same quality on set 2 as set 1 = you are game-5 ready
🧠 Mental — During RSA

Process cue only: During every rep: focus ONLY on the next corner. Not the score, not how tired you are — just the next corner. This is the same present-moment focus used by Farag in game 5 tiebreaks. Metric: Focus breaks per RSA set (target: 0)

TUESDAY Gym B — Upper body + posterior chain + Nordic curls 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–65 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–9  ·  Nordic curls this week: 3×8 reps (5s + quality). Lower as slowly as you can. Non-negotiable.

Warm-up

— · 8 min
WHY: Posterior chain + shoulder activation.
  • 5 min easy rower
  • Band pull-aparts × 15
  • Shoulder ER band × 10 each side
  • Hip hinge drill × 8 (push hips back, feel hamstrings)
  • Single-leg calf raise × 8 each side, slow

DB Romanian Deadlift (RDL)

3 sets × 10 reps · 2 min rest · Tempo: 3-0-1 · 40–45kg+ each hand
WHY: Trains hamstring in lengthened position — exactly when it tears during sprinting and COD. 3s eccentric is where the adaptation happens.
  • Hinge at hip — push hips BACK first, not down
  • Soft bend in knees — this is NOT a squat
  • 3s lowering — feel full hamstring stretch at bottom
  • Neutral spine throughout — do not round lower back
  • Heavier each week — log it

Single-leg RDL (DB balance)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 90s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Proprioception + hamstring under single-leg balance challenge. Mirrors on-court landing mechanics directly.
  • Balance on one leg — soft knee
  • Hinge forward, feel hamstring of standing leg
  • Hold DB on opposite side to standing leg
  • Ankle of standing leg is training too — stay stable

Nordic hamstring curl

3×8 reps (5s + quality) · 90s rest · 4–5 second lowering
WHY: Most evidence-based injury prevention exercise. Reduces hamstring injury 57–70%. Non-negotiable — this is a 16-week progression to 3×8 unassisted.
  • Knees on pad, ankles held by bench or partner
  • Lower AS SLOWLY AS POSSIBLE — 4–5 full seconds
  • Catch at 45° — use hands only as needed
  • Full reset between reps
  • NEVER add reps beyond progression — the DOMS is severe

Pull-up (or assisted machine)

4 sets × 6–8 reps · 2 min rest
WHY: Lat + bicep + scapular control. Pulling volume must exceed pressing — squash swings create chronic internal rotation dominance.
  • Dead hang start — full arm extension
  • Pull chest toward bar (not chin)
  • 2s controlled lowering
  • If 0 pull-ups: assisted machine until you can do 3 unassisted

Cable face pull

3 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Slow
WHY: Posterior deltoid + external rotators. Counteracts chronic internal rotation from squash swings. Shoulder longevity.
  • Pull to NOSE height — not chin, not forehead
  • Elbows flare HIGH and WIDE at end
  • Slow controlled throughout
  • Light weight — this is health work

Landmine press (or kneeling DB press)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest
WHY: Scapula-friendly pressing. Maintains pushing strength without impingement risk.
  • Kneeling = trunk stability challenge
  • Press at 45° angle
  • Do not arch lower back

Cable rotational chop (high → low)

3 sets × 6 reps/side · 60s rest · Light weight, fast concentric
WHY: Rotational power chain — hip-to-trunk force transfer for every squash shot. Mirrors the forehand drive plane.
  • Light-moderate weight — FAST concentric
  • Drive from back hip first
  • Controlled return (3s)
  • Progress weight only when speed stays fast

Soleus ISO (seated, knee bent 90°)ANKLE

3 sets × 35s each side · 30s rest
WHY: Primary Achilles tendon loading position. Gold standard for Achilles prevention with ankle injury history.
  • Knee bent ~90° — this is critical
  • Maximum force into floor
  • Hold 35s — do not reduce effort
  • Progress: bodyweight → DB on knee

Tibialis raise (against wall)ANKLE

2 sets × 15–20 reps · 30s rest
WHY: Tibialis anterior strength. Controls foot position on court. Prevents shin splints.
  • Back flat against wall
  • Lift toes as high as possible
  • Controlled lowering
  • Feel the shin working
🧠 Mental — After session (10 min)

Post-session journal: Write 3 things: (1) What went well physically or mentally. (2) One specific improvement. (3) 'I am building the kind of player who...' sentence. Identity reinforcement builds confidence from evidence, not wishful thinking. Metric: Confidence rating after session (1–10)

WEDNESDAY Court — COD + ghosting + RSA 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 45–50 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–8  ·  Ankle brace mandatory for all court sessions through week 14. Log corner count metric every session.

Movement prep

— · 10 min
  • Ankle prep
  • Technical ghost 5 min at 60%
  • 3 build-up court sprints

Elite COD sequence

3 sets · 45s between sets
WHY: Full COD quality battery. Every rep: braking quality first.
  • Decel to stick × 3 each side
  • 45° cuts × 4 each side
  • 180° T-junction turns × 3
  • Reactive cut on partner cue (or self-call) × 3

Threshold ghost — 5-game simulation

3 sets × 4 min on / 90s easy
WHY: VT2 threshold in squash-specific movement. Trains game 3–5 physiological state.
  • RPE 7–8 sustained — not sprinting
  • Count corner touches per minute
  • Same quality on set 3 as set 1 = game-5 ready
  • Log the corner count — it's your fitness KPI

REACTIVE ghost — partner or self

2 sets × 2 min · 60s rest
WHY: Elite reactive agility under fatigue. Replicates game-5 decision-making.
  • Partner calls corners OR self-call randomly
  • Eyes up — anticipate early
  • Add: wrong cue = don't move (decision quality training)
  • Fast, decisive, accurate

Narrow-wide attention switching: NARROW focus during each sprint to corner. WIDE tactical focus on T return. Practice switching deliberately. This is the attention skill that separates elite from world-class. Metric: Focus quality 1–10 (honest self-rating)

THURSDAY Prehab + Ankle rehab 🏋 Gym🏊 Pool ▾

Ankle alphabet — active ROMANKLE

2 sets · A–Z each foot · Slow and deliberate
WHY: Restores full talocrural joint ROM. Grade-3 sprain = scar tissue restriction. Work through every direction.
  • Seated, foot off floor
  • Write A through Z slowly
  • Feel resistance in some directions — work through gently
  • Always first, before any weight-bearing ankle work

Single-leg balanceANKLE

3 sets × 45s · Perturbation (partner pushes) · Sport-specific challenge
WHY: Rebuilds ankle mechanoreceptors destroyed by grade-3 sprain. Balance training reduces ankle re-injury ~46%. Research-backed.
  • Soft knee — never locked
  • Focus on fixed point on wall
  • Progress challenge each week — eyes open is too easy after week 4
  • Grade-3 sprain = destroyed proprioception. You are re-training neural feedback.

3 sets × 12 reps/side · 30s rest · Slow
WHY: Grade-3 sprains damage peroneal tendons — your lateral ankle stabilisers. This rebuilds them eccentrically. Without this, re-injury risk stays elevated indefinitely.
  • Band around front of foot, anchored to the side
  • Evert foot outward against resistance
  • Control the return SLOWLY — that is the eccentric
  • Feel outside of lower leg working

Eccentric calf raise on stepANKLE

3 sets × 12 reps · 45s rest · 3–5s lowering · Single-leg
WHY: Eccentric loading = gold standard for Achilles tendon health. Progressive load from bilateral to single-leg.
  • Rise on TWO feet at top
  • Lower on ONE foot — 3–5s slow descent
  • Full heel drop below step at bottom
  • If ankle pain >2/5: bilateral lowering only

Lateral hop and stickANKLEPhase 3+

3 sets × 5 hops/side · 45s rest · Hold stick 3s
WHY: Phase 3 ankle: dynamic lateral loading. Recreates lateral ankle stress of squash COD.
  • Hop laterally 20–30cm
  • Land SINGLE-LEG — stick for 3 full seconds
  • No ankle wobble = ready to progress
  • If ankle pain >2/5: bilateral hop only

Shoulder prehab circuit

3 rounds · Short rest between exercises
WHY: Rotator cuff health — every squash swing decelerates eccentrically through the cuff.
  • Band ER × 15 each side
  • Face pull × 15
  • Prone Y raise × 10
  • Band pull-apart × 15

Copenhagen plank (groin + adductor)

2 sets × 45s+ each side · Long lever (ankle on bench)
WHY: Adductor strength = documented groin injury prevention. Copenhagen plank is the most specific adductor loading tool.
  • Top leg on bench, bottom leg free
  • Hips SQUARE — do not rotate
  • Short lever first (4–6 weeks), then progress to long lever
  • Progress 5s each week

Hip 90/90 stretch + active reps

2 sets × 45s hold + 8 active reps/side
WHY: Hip rotation mobility needed for lunge depth and shot production.
  • Passive hold first
  • Then: lift front shin, hold 3s, lower × 8
  • Builds strength at end range — not just passive flexibility
🧠 Mental — Throughout prehab session

Intentional breathing: Breathe 4-2-6 (inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s) through EVERY exercise. Prehab becomes a breathing meditation. Lowers cortisol, builds parasympathetic activation — the same state needed for clutch points. Metric: Did breathing feel more natural by the end? Yes/No

FRIDAY Speed — ATP-PCr track session 🏃 Track ▾

Duration: 30–35 min  ·  Intensity: Max on sprint reps  ·  ANKLE BRACE MANDATORY. 3 min rest between EVERY rep — the rest IS the training. This is not conditioning.

Dynamic warm-up

10 min
WHY: Neural priming — cannot sprint well without it.
  • High knees × 20
  • Heel flicks × 20
  • Lateral shuffle 5m × 4
  • Hip openers × 10/side
  • 3 × 20m build-up at 70/85/95%
  • Ankle alphabet × 1 each foot

6-second MAX acceleration

12 reps · 1 rep each · 3 min FULL rest between EVERY rep
WHY: 6s sprint = 94% ATP-PCr energy. 3 min rest = >95% PCr resynthesised. This trains alactic power AND aerobic PCr resynthesis rate = game-5 first-step speed.
  • From squash ready position: crouch, weight forward, feet split
  • Drive back leg through floor — first step is everything
  • MAXIMUM effort — 100%, not 95%
  • Stop at 6 seconds — do not continue
  • 3 min timer between every rep — use sidebar
  • Stop session if any rep feels >10% slower than your best rep

10m acceleration

5 reps · 3–4 min full rest each
WHY: First-step + continuation speed development.
  • Same mechanics — push through to 10m
  • Full explosive push from start
  • Full recovery every rep — same quality rules apply

Lateral first-step (from T-stance, 2m)

4 sets × 3 reps/side · 90s rest
WHY: Most squash sprints are lateral. This is the exact first step in every rally.
  • From T-position crouch: split stance
  • Explosive lateral first step — drive opposite hip
  • Stop at 2m — hold position 1s
  • This trains the most common first movement in squash
🧠 Mental — Speed session — embedded

Process focus during sprints: Focus ONLY on your first step during every sprint — not the time, not how you feel, not what's next. Just the first step. This trains the process focus that prevents overthinking under pressure. Metric: Did you think about anything else during the sprint? Aim for No.

SATURDAY Full rest + weekly debrief 😴 Rest ▾

Duration: 0 min  ·  Intensity: Complete recovery  ·  Zero structured training. Light walk only. Eat full macros — do not restrict on rest days. Every adaptation from this week happens TODAY.

🧠 Mental — Saturday evening (20 min)

Weekly review — full debrief: Answer with brutal honesty: (1) Hit every session? If not — why? (2) Are weights going up? (3) Am I better at one specific court thing? (4) Rate mental skills 1–10: focus, confidence, composure, resilience, motivation. (5) Sleep average this week? (6) One thing to do differently. (7) One thing I did well. This review IS training. Metric: Overall mental performance this week 1–10. Trending up from last week?

Week 39 focus: Systematic progression. Log all numbers. What measured improves, what measured stagnates tells you what to fix.

SUNDAY Gym A — Lower body + plyometrics 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–70 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–9  ·  Plyometrics ALWAYS first when fresh. Never under fatigue. Log every weight.

Warm-up

— · 10 min
WHY: Full neural + ankle priming before loading.
  • 5 min easy bike or rower
  • Ankle alphabet: draw A–Z with each foot while seated × 1 each
  • Hip 90/90 mobility × 45s each side
  • Glute bridge × 10 (activate glutes)
  • Dead bug × 6 each side, slow
  • Bodyweight split squat × 5 each side — test ankle response

Lateral bound + stick landingLevel 4 Plyo

4 sets × 4/side · 90s rest
WHY: Level 4 horizontal power + deceleration. The squash push-off in isolation. Stick landing builds deceleration strength directly.
  • Drive explosively off outside foot
  • Land single-leg — stick for 2 full seconds
  • The 2-second hold IS the training — do not cheat it
  • Ankle brace on if any residual instability

Leg press (machine)

4 sets × 6 reps · 2–3 min rest · Tempo: 3-0-X · ~308kg target
WHY: Primary quad builder. 3-second eccentric builds tendon stiffness — more important than the weight right now in block 1. Log weight every session.
  • Full range — all the way down (3s)
  • Feet shoulder-width, toes 10–15° out
  • Drive through heels — not toes
  • Last 2 reps should feel hard (RPE 7–8) after block 1

DB RFESS — Rear foot elevated split squat

4 sets × 4–5 reps/side · 2 min rest · Tempo: 3-1-X · 38–42kg each hand
WHY: Most squash-specific gym exercise. Rear foot elevated = quad + glute loaded in deep lunge position, exactly what every squash shot demands. This exercise never leaves your program.
  • Rear foot on bench (15–25cm height)
  • Front foot far enough forward — front shin vertical at bottom
  • 3s eccentric — own the bottom for 1s — explosive drive up
  • Front heel NEVER lifts
  • Drive back leg into bench for stability
  • Log the DB weight every session — it must go up

Glute bridge (BW or DB on hips)

3 sets × 12 reps · 60s rest
WHY: Hip extension power for every push-off on court. Zero ankle stress.
  • Pause 2s at top — squeeze glutes hard
  • Drive through heels
  • Do not use lower back — glutes only

Lateral lunge (DB)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest · Tempo: 3-2-1
WHY: Squash is laterally dominant. Lateral lunge loads adductors and quads in the wide positions your body takes on court.
  • Step directly sideways — deep lunge
  • 3s into position, 2s hold — feel adductors under load
  • Keep non-lunging leg straight
  • Drive back through mid-foot

Hip thrust (machine or DB on hips)

3 sets × 10 reps · 75s rest
WHY: Glute strength for sprint mechanics and court push-off.
  • Full hip extension at top — pause 1s
  • Ribs down, chin tucked
  • Drive through heels

Seated calf raise (machine or DB on knee)ANKLE

4 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Soleus loading = Achilles protection. Bent knee specifically loads the soleus. Non-negotiable with ankle history — start day 1 and never stop.
  • 3s lowering — this is where the adaptation is
  • Full range up AND down
  • Feel the stretch at the bottom of every rep

Standing calf raise (machine or heavy DB)

4 sets × 10 reps · 60s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Gastrocnemius strength. Standing = ankle in extension = explosive push-off position.
  • 3s lowering
  • Should struggle by rep 8
  • Straight knee throughout

Pallof press (cable or band)

3 sets × 10 reps/side · 45s rest
WHY: Anti-rotation core stiffness. Squash demands both producing AND resisting rotation.
  • Press out — hold 1s — return
  • Zero trunk rotation — that IS the exercise
  • Stand perpendicular to cable/band anchor

Dead bug

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 45s rest · Slow
WHY: Lumbar stabilisation under limb loading. Foundation of all core work.
  • Lower back GLUED to floor — if it lifts, reduce range
  • Opposite arm + leg extend simultaneously
  • Exhale as you extend
🧠 Mental — Before session (5 min)

Box breathing + intention: 5 cycles: inhale 4s → hold 2s → exhale 6s. Then say aloud: 'Today I build the foundation of my strength. Every rep is technique.' Start this now — it programs pre-competition composure. Metric: Anxiety 1–10 before session (note in log)

MONDAY RSA — Match simulation 🎾 Court ▾

Duration: 50 min  ·  Intensity: Very high  ·  Hardest conditioning session of the week. This IS 5-game fitness training.

Warm-up

12 min
  • Easy ghosting
  • Footwork patterns
  • 3 court build-up sprints

Match-simulation RSA

3 sets × 8–10 reps · 20s rest between reps · 5 min between sets
WHY: RSA training directly trains the ATP-PCr + aerobic interface. Identical sprint quality in rep 10 as rep 1 = game-5 quality maintained.
  • Each rep: full 6-corner ghost at MAXIMUM speed
  • 20s rest = between-point rest in squash — intentionally short
  • Stop a set when slowest rep >8% slower than fastest
  • Log: fastest and slowest rep each set
  • 5 min between sets is mandatory — use the timer

Threshold ghost

2 sets × 4 min on / 90s easy
WHY: Simulates game 3–5 physiological state.
  • RPE 7–8 sustained — not sprinting
  • Count corner touches per minute — log this
  • Same quality on set 2 as set 1 = you are game-5 ready
🧠 Mental — During RSA

Process cue only: During every rep: focus ONLY on the next corner. Not the score, not how tired you are — just the next corner. This is the same present-moment focus used by Farag in game 5 tiebreaks. Metric: Focus breaks per RSA set (target: 0)

TUESDAY Gym B — Upper body + posterior chain + Nordic curls 🏋 Gym ▾

Duration: 55–65 min  ·  Intensity: RPE 7–9  ·  Nordic curls this week: 3×8 reps (5s + quality). Lower as slowly as you can. Non-negotiable.

Warm-up

— · 8 min
WHY: Posterior chain + shoulder activation.
  • 5 min easy rower
  • Band pull-aparts × 15
  • Shoulder ER band × 10 each side
  • Hip hinge drill × 8 (push hips back, feel hamstrings)
  • Single-leg calf raise × 8 each side, slow

DB Romanian Deadlift (RDL)

3 sets × 10 reps · 2 min rest · Tempo: 3-0-1 · 40–45kg+ each hand
WHY: Trains hamstring in lengthened position — exactly when it tears during sprinting and COD. 3s eccentric is where the adaptation happens.
  • Hinge at hip — push hips BACK first, not down
  • Soft bend in knees — this is NOT a squat
  • 3s lowering — feel full hamstring stretch at bottom
  • Neutral spine throughout — do not round lower back
  • Heavier each week — log it

Single-leg RDL (DB balance)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 90s rest · Tempo: 3-0-1
WHY: Proprioception + hamstring under single-leg balance challenge. Mirrors on-court landing mechanics directly.
  • Balance on one leg — soft knee
  • Hinge forward, feel hamstring of standing leg
  • Hold DB on opposite side to standing leg
  • Ankle of standing leg is training too — stay stable

Nordic hamstring curl

3×8 reps (5s + quality) · 90s rest · 4–5 second lowering
WHY: Most evidence-based injury prevention exercise. Reduces hamstring injury 57–70%. Non-negotiable — this is a 16-week progression to 3×8 unassisted.
  • Knees on pad, ankles held by bench or partner
  • Lower AS SLOWLY AS POSSIBLE — 4–5 full seconds
  • Catch at 45° — use hands only as needed
  • Full reset between reps
  • NEVER add reps beyond progression — the DOMS is severe

Pull-up (or assisted machine)

4 sets × 6–8 reps · 2 min rest
WHY: Lat + bicep + scapular control. Pulling volume must exceed pressing — squash swings create chronic internal rotation dominance.
  • Dead hang start — full arm extension
  • Pull chest toward bar (not chin)
  • 2s controlled lowering
  • If 0 pull-ups: assisted machine until you can do 3 unassisted

Cable face pull

3 sets × 15 reps · 60s rest · Slow
WHY: Posterior deltoid + external rotators. Counteracts chronic internal rotation from squash swings. Shoulder longevity.
  • Pull to NOSE height — not chin, not forehead
  • Elbows flare HIGH and WIDE at end
  • Slow controlled throughout
  • Light weight — this is health work

Landmine press (or kneeling DB press)

3 sets × 8 reps/side · 75s rest
WHY: Scapula-friendly pressing. Maintains pushing strength without impingement risk.
  • Kneeling = trunk stability challenge
  • Press at 45° angle
  • Do not arch lower back

Cable rotational chop (high → low)

3 sets × 6 reps/side · 60s rest · Light weight, fast concentric
WHY: Rotational power chain — hip-to-trunk force transfer for every squash shot. Mirrors the forehand drive plane.
  • Light-moderate weight — FAST concentric
  • Drive from back hip first
  • Controlled return (3s)
  • Progress weight only when speed stays fast

Soleus ISO (seated, knee bent 90°)ANKLE

3 sets × 35s each side · 30s rest
WHY: Primary Achilles tendon loading position. Gold standard for Achilles prevention with ankle injury history.
  • Knee bent ~90° — this is critical
  • Maximum force into floor
  • Hold 35s — do not reduce effort
  • Progress: bodyweight → DB on knee

Tibialis raise (against wall)ANKLE

2 sets × 15–20 reps · 30s rest
WHY: Tibialis anterior strength. Controls foot position on court. Prevents shin splints.
  • Back flat against wall
  • Lift toes as high as possible
  • Controlled lowering
  • Feel the shin working
🧠 Mental — After session (10 min)
